title: eriogonum sphaerocephalum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum sphaerocephalum subsp. typicum, also known as the globemallow, is a perennial shrub in the buckwheat family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The globemallow has been used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, and it is also used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, allowing for various methods of consumption and application, showcasing its versatility in traditional herbal medicine.
